TGT: Reynolds Wrap Introduces New Hearts Fun Foil, Adding a Heartfelt Touch to Everyday Moments. Reynolds Wrap has launched a new "Hearts Fun Foil," an embossed aluminum foil featuring a playful heart design intended to add a thoughtful touch to everyday kitchen activities and gestures. This new product is a permanent addition to the Fun Foil lineup and aims to help consumers elevate heartfelt gestures year-round, whether for gifting food, covering dishes, or sending leftovers. It is available nationwide at major retailers like Walmart, Target, and Amazon.

Is Ulta’s Messi Fragrances And Virtue Labs Push Altering The Investment Case For Ulta Beauty (ULTA)?. Ulta Beauty is expanding its partnerships with Virtue Labs and launching Messi Fragrances exclusively, utilizing its physical footprint and omni-channel reach. While these moves strengthen Ulta's role as a launch platform for beauty brands and enhance its haircare assortment, they do not fundamentally change the investment focus on preserving margins and offsetting rising store costs or the eventual loss of the Target partnership. Analysts project Ulta's revenue to reach $13.8 billion and earnings to hit $1.3 billion by 2028, with a fair value estimate suggesting a 27% upside.

Target Celebrates Pokémon Anniversary with Limited-Time Collection. Target Corporation is launching an exclusive, limited-time Pokémon collection to celebrate the franchise's 30th anniversary, becoming the only U.S.-based mass retailer to partner with The Pokémon Co. Int’l for this milestone. The collection will feature over 100 items across various categories, with nearly half priced under $20, and will roll out in two phases starting May 2. The initiative includes a social-first marketing campaign featuring Pokémon fans and creators, along with immersive in-store experiences and giveaways at select Target locations.

The S&P 500 Index saw a modest increase during the same trading session.
SBUX: Starbucks Stock in 2026: Buy the Dip or Sell the Rally as Turnaround Gains Traction?. Starbucks stock has risen 15% year-to-date in 2026, reaching $98, driven by early signs of a turnaround under new CEO Brian Niccol. The "Back to Starbucks" strategy, which includes menu simplification and improved store operations, has led to positive U.S. transaction growth. However, challenges persist in the China market and from cautious consumers, necessitating strong execution in the coming months to validate sustained growth for the premium valuation.

Starbucks Launches ChatGPT Beta App To Inspire Drink Orders. Starbucks has launched a beta version of an app within ChatGPT, allowing customers to find and customize drink orders using generative AI by typing "@Starbucks." This initiative is part of Starbucks' "Back to Starbucks" strategy to boost customer engagement, especially among younger demographics, and follows their previous AI integration with Microsoft's Azure OpenAI platform. While customization and location selection are available through the ChatGPT app, final purchases still need to be completed via the Starbucks app or website to maintain loyalty program integrity.

Starbucks investors get $0.62 a share as May 29 payout is set. Starbucks Corporation announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.62 per share of outstanding Common Stock. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on May 29, 2026, to shareholders of record as of May 15, 2026. The announcement highlights Starbucks' commitment to returning value to its shareholders.
DIS: The Walt Disney Company $DIS Stake Raised by Farther Finance Advisors LLC. Farther Finance Advisors LLC increased its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 10.2% in the fourth quarter, bringing their total holdings to 70,551 shares valued at approximately $8.03 million. Disney is implementing strategic changes including job cuts, investing in Epic Games, and integrating Disney+ and Hulu, which analysts believe could improve margins despite potential execution and legal risks. The company currently holds a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $132.19, trading near $103.

Disney to cut 1,000 jobs as CEO announces layoffs across company. Walt Disney Company's new CEO, Josh D’Amaro, has announced plans to cut 1,000 jobs across various departments, including marketing, studio and television business, ESPN, and corporate functions. This decision aims to streamline operations and adapt to changes in the entertainment industry, which include a declining television market, shrinking box office revenues, and increased competition. The layoffs follow a larger round in 2023 when Disney eliminated 7,000 positions to save costs and improve financial performance.

The Walt Disney Company begins 1,000 job cuts across studios, ESPN, tech. The Walt Disney Company is implementing a restructuring that includes eliminating 1,000 jobs across its studios, ESPN, tech, and corporate divisions. This move, announced by new CEO Josh D’Amaro, aims to streamline operations and consolidate marketing efforts into a unified enterprise marketing organization. The cuts represent approximately 0.4% of Disney's global workforce and are intended to foster a more agile and technologically-enabled company.

Marvel Studios Cuts 8% of Staff As The Walt Disney Co. CEO Josh D’Amaro Pushes ‘Technologically-Enabled Workforce To Meet Tomorrow’s Needs’. Marvel Studios is undergoing an 8% staff reduction as part of a larger layoff initiative by new Walt Disney Co. CEO Josh D’Amaro. These cuts, affecting around 1,000 roles across Disney's media holdings, aim to streamline operations and foster a more agile, technologically-enabled workforce. The affected positions at Marvel span film and TV production, comics, franchises, finance, and legal, also significantly impacting the visual development team.
NKE: EXEC: Nike and Apple CEOs Loaded Up on NKE Shares as Price Fell. Nike's president and CEO, Elliott Hill, and Apple's CEO and Nike's lead independent director, Tim Cook, recently purchased significant amounts of Nike shares after a stock price decline. Cook bought 25,000 shares for approximately $1.06 million, while Hill acquired 23,660 shares for about $1 million. These purchases are seen by some investors as a vote of confidence amid Nike's disappointing guidance and a significant stock drop.
